mainmatter.p: A monoidal category is a category C equipped with the following structure and axioms:

ol.li: a functor C \times C \xrightarrow { \otimes }C called the tensor product;
an object I \in C called the tensor unit;

a natural isomorphism { \mathopen {} \left ( x \otimes y \right ) \mathclose {}} \otimes z \xrightarrow { \alpha _{ x,y,z } } x \otimes { \mathopen {} \left ( y \otimes z \right ) \mathclose {}} called the associator;
a natural isomorphism I \otimes x \xrightarrow { \lambda _{ x } }x called the left unitor;
a natural isomorphism x \otimes I \xrightarrow { \rho _{ x } }x called the right unitor;

mainmatter.p: Let { \mathopen {} \left ( V,I, \otimes , \alpha , \lambda , \rho \right ) \mathclose {}} be a monoidal category. A category C enriched in V is defined by the following structure and axioms:

ol.li: for any two objects x,y, an object \mathbf {hom} _{ C } { \mathopen {} \left ( x , y \right ) \mathclose {}} \in V;
for every object x, a morphism I \xrightarrow {i _{ x } } \mathbf {hom} _{ C } { \mathopen {} \left ( x , x \right ) \mathclose {}} in V;

for all objects x,y,z, a morphism \mathbf {hom} _{ C } { \mathopen {} \left ( x , y \right ) \mathclose {}} \otimes \mathbf {hom} _{ C } { \mathopen {} \left ( y , z \right ) \mathclose {}} \xrightarrow {c _{ x,y,z } } \mathbf {hom} _{ C } { \mathopen {} \left ( x , z \right ) \mathclose {}} in V.

frontmatter.title: Monoidal categories are bicategories with a single object

mainmatter.p: A monoidal category is nothing more than a bicategory with only a single object; to be more precise, given a such a bicategory B with just one object \bullet \in B, the hom category \mathbf {hom} _{ B } { \mathopen {} \left ( \bullet , \bullet \right ) \mathclose {}} is monoidal, with the tensor product given by horizontal composition, the tensor unit given by the horizontal identity, and unitors and associators given by those of B.
